text:
*[Bug 17549] When building a package, use inRbuildignore() before copying package contents to Tdir*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=17549>

--- Comment #15 from Kurt Hornik (<mailto:Kurt.Hornik@wu.ac.at|Kurt.Hornik@wu.ac.at>) ---
A quick comment.
IIrc, when I wrote the code many years ago the idea was to prepare everything
into a temp location and then tar up ignored the things to be ignored as per
WRE, including what gets matched via .Rbuildignore.
We could of course change the semantics (and ask maintainers to clean up
themselves if preparing added unwanted things), or add a way of saying please
ignore me when preparing, but perhaps we can salvage most of the possible
speedups without (much) changing the semantics?
So let me ask again: perhaps it suffices to exclude top level subdirs in
.Rbuildignore when preparing?  
As I wrote, hard to assess the benefit by looking at what we have on CRAN, but
one could perhaps look at what people have on codeberg or github?
